input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues, both common:

**1. `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource`**

`StaticResource` resolves **once** at load time and never updates. Switch to `DynamicResource` so the binding re-evaluates when the dictionary changes:

```xml
<!-- ✗ Won't update at runtime -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✓ Updates when you swap the merged dictionary -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes in code-behind like:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(useDark ? new DarkTheme() : new LightTheme());
```

**2. Activity restart on Android config change**

Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` when `uiMode` changes. Tell it not to by adding `uiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges` in your `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges =
        ConfigChanges.ScreenSize |
        ConfigChanges.Orientation |
        ConfigChanges.UiMode |          // ← add this
        ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout |
        ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize |
        ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

This tells Android your app handles the UI-mode change itself, preventing the full restart. You can then react to it programmatically:

```csharp
// In MainActivity
public override void OnConfigurationChanged(Configuration newConfig)
{
    base.OnConfigurationChanged(newConfig);

    bool isDark = (newConfig.UiMode & UiMode.NightMask) == UiMode.NightYes;
    // swap your merged dictionary here
}
```

**TL;DR:** `StaticResource` = compile-once, `DynamicResource` = reactive. And add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to stop the activity restart.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two specific issues with dark mode in a .NET MAUI Android app:

1. **StaticResource doesn't update at runtime** — This is a classic MAUI/WPF/Xamarin issue. `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time. To get runtime updates, they need to use `DynamicResource` instead.

2. **App restarts when Android dark mode toggles** — By default, Android recreates the Activity when the UI mode (dark/light) changes. In MAUI, you need to handle the `configChanges` for `uiMode` in the Android manifest to prevent the activity from being destroyed and recreated.


Now I'll put together a clear solution addressing both problems.
